lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings once again, honourable [player name/Fremennik name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you all going to do now? Are you finally going to move to another location?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: No, I've decided to set up a permanent camp here.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: There is no need to move now with our new supply of food. Also, there is a protective spirit looking after us now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Excuse me, have you seen my helmet?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And what helmet would that be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The one from the bear man thing person... you know!

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Oh, yes... as a matter of fact we did find such a helmet inside the cave.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We had a look in there after you killed the abomination that used to live inside.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Here, take it. You should take proper care of this, trophies from your enemy are an important keepsake.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all very interesting but I must be off again.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Safe journey, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What exactly happened to your daughter?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: I cannot exactly say. I believe some of her spirit must have fused with the pool she loved so much. She is no longer my daughter, but the daughter of the mountain.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: We will stay here, under her protection. With our new food supplies we will be able to stay here for many years more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you didn't believe in magic?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: How little do you understand our ways. Of course we believe in magic, we merely believe it is not for the living to wield it.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Many spirits are connected with the earth or the water. There is the Draugen, for instance, a malevolent spirit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, I ran into him.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Have you? We do not approve of the Fremennik hunt, the Draugen should be left alone, evil though it is.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: And perhaps you have also come across the Fossegrimen?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I have.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Then there is an ancient water spirit that the Fremennik call the Fossegrimen.

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Now that is a spirit that is quite different from the Draugen... she is, perhaps, not entirely dissimilar to the Mountain Dottir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The mountain what?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: You would call her mountain daughter. Asleif is no longer my daughter, she belongs to the mountains now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think you're all mad. You, the guy in the bearsuit, Ragnar... you're all completely mad! I think the cold got to your brains!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean, your daughter was missing for 14 years and you still thought she was just missing! And you ignored and walled in the bearman because you thought he was a god!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you people thinking?

lines.speaker: Hamal the Chieftain
lines.text: Yet you participated in what you call madness, outerlander. You are perhaps not so different from any of us.
